title: proto.py: Enum conversion raises ValueError on unknown protocol values
description:
File: python/src/lichen/interface/meshtastic/proto.py
Lines: 141, 376

The protobuf decoder raises ValueError when encountering unknown enum values:
1. Line 141: RoutingError(val) for unknown routing error codes
2. Line 376: PacketPriority(val) for unknown priority values

Protocol buffers are designed to be forward-compatible - unknown enum values should be preserved or handled gracefully, not cause exceptions. This will crash the decoder when a newer Meshtastic client sends messages with new enum values.

Impact: Receiving messages from newer Meshtastic clients with new priority or routing error codes will crash the parser, causing DoS.

Fix: Wrap enum construction in try/except and use a default value (e.g., RoutingError.NONE, PacketPriority.DEFAULT) or store the raw integer and add is_unknown() method.
close_reason: Fixed: Wrapped enum construction in try/except at lines 141 and 376. Unknown RoutingError values now default to NONE, unknown PacketPriority values default to DEFAULT. This provides forward compatibility with newer Meshtastic clients.
